title: Fixed full-screen drawer collapses into the header instead of covering the viewport
summary: A position:fixed inset:0 drawer nested inside a header with backdrop-filter gets sized to the header box, not the viewport, because filter/backdrop-filter/transform ancestors become the containing block for fixed descendants.
mistake: The agent built a mobile drawer as position:fixed; inset:0 inside a sticky header that had backdrop-filter:blur(10px). Per CSS spec, backdrop-filter (like filter, transform, perspective, will-change:transform) makes that ancestor the containing block for fixed-position descendants. The 'full-screen' overlay was silently laid out inside the ~70px header strip: its background covered only the header band and its links overflowed on top of the page content. Screenshots looked like a z-index or opacity bug, hiding the real cause.
instead: Before implementing a fixed overlay, walk its ancestor chain for containing-block creators: in DevTools run [...ancestors].map(el => getComputedStyle(el)).find(cs => cs.backdropFilter !== 'none' || cs.filter !== 'none' || cs.transform !== 'none'). If the header needs blur, either (a) disable it in the mobile media query and use a solid rgba background instead, or (b) move the drawer node out to <body>. Verify numerically, not visually: assert drawer.getBoundingClientRect() equals {top:0, left:0, width:innerWidth, height:innerHeight} when open.
